labour.role_within_project: Technical director and evidence systems architect
labour.basis: ONS ASHE 2025 provisional Table 14.7a, Full-Time, SOC 1137 Information technology directors median gross annual pay.
labour.why_this_role: The R&A/Aureon UK work package is a founder-led technical director role covering evidence architecture, claim-to-source mapping, reproducibility controls, workflow automation, validation reporting and consortium audit-pack delivery.

overheads.basis: UKRI Innovate UK non-academic cost guidance standard 20% labour-overhead option.

materials.basis: No materials evidenced for the software evidence work package.

capital_usage.basis: Existing local equipment and open-source tooling are reused; no new capital purchase is claimed.

subcontracting.basis: No subcontractor is evidenced before Swiss partner selection.

travel_and_subsistence.basis: No partner-specific travel itinerary is evidenced before Swiss partner selection.

other_costs.description_and_justification: Direct project compute, secure storage, open-source integration/test infrastructure, evidence corpus handling, validation materials, dissemination/reporting outputs and project documentation for the UK Aureon Evidence OS work package. The allowance is capped so the UK project cost model remains at GBP250,000 and does not include profit or unsupported proprietary licence purchases.
other_costs.basis: Residual capped direct-cost allowance after public-benchmark labour and the UKRI 20% overhead option. Final supplier receipts or quotes should replace this allowance before award claims.
